# SKILL_D100_report_builder — D100 Phase 2 Content Generator

## Role

You generate the Phase 2 content for a Dream 100 prospect: keywords, Google Ad campaigns, patient email sequences, and health assessment configuration. This content is saved as `phase2_output.json` and injected into the D100 HTML template by `scripts/inject_report.py`.

**You do NOT write HTML.** The HTML is auto-generated by the Python template injector from `templates/seo_report_template.html`. Your job is to produce the JSON data that populates it.

---

## Architecture (v3.2 — Template-Based)

```
Phase 1 (Python runner)
  → phase1_data.json  (SEMrush, scrape, brand colors, crawl)

Phase 2 (You — Claude Code native)
  → phase2_output.json  (keywords, ads, emails, app_config)

Phase 3 (Python runner: inject_report.py)
  → reads phase1_data.json + phase2_output.json
  → fills {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens in templates/seo_report_template.html
  → writes index.html (175–200KB, fully personalized)
  → deploys to Vercel → hbs-{company-slug}.vercel.app
```

**Token usage:** ~18K (down from ~136K). Build time: <5 seconds.

---

## Input Files (read both before generating)

- `phase1_data.json` — SEMrush data, website scrape, brand colors, crawl intel, booking URL, logo URL
- *(No other input needed)*

---

## Output

Write `phase2_output.json` to the same run directory.

---

## phase2_output.json Schema

```json
{
  "keywords": ["keyword 1", "keyword 2", ...],  // 100 keywords total

  "ads": [
    {
      "campaign": "Campaign Name — Benefit Focus",
      "headlines": ["Headline 1", "Headline 2", ...],  // 10-15 headlines, ≤30 chars each
      "descriptions": ["Description 1", "Description 2", ...],  // 4-6 descriptions, ≤90 chars each
      "keywords": ["keyword 1", "keyword 2", ...],  // 15-25 exact-match keywords for this campaign
      "extensions": {
        "sitelinks": ["Sitelink 1", "Sitelink 2", "Sitelink 3", "Sitelink 4"],
        "callouts": ["Callout 1", "Callout 2", "Callout 3"]
      }
    }
  ],  // exactly 3 campaigns

  "emails": [
    {
      "type": "value_drop",
      "subject": "Subject line here",
      "preview": "Email preview text here",
      "body": "Full email body here\n\nParagraphs separated by double newline.\n\nSignature at bottom."
    },
    {
      "type": "mechanism_story",
      "subject": "...",
      "preview": "...",
      "body": "..."
    },
    {
      "type": "proof_results",
      "subject": "...",
      "preview": "...",
      "body": "..."
    }
  ],

  "app_config": {
    "practice_name": "Full Practice Name",
    "doctor_name": "Dr. First Last, Credentials",
    "tagline": "Short tagline for assessment app",
    "primary_color": "#XXXXXX",
    "accent_color": "#XXXXXX",
    "concerns": [
      "Condition Name 1",
      "Condition Name 2",
      ...
    ],
    "symptom_map": {
      "Condition Name 1": [
        "Symptom description 1",
        "Symptom description 2",
        "Symptom description 3",
        "Symptom description 4",
        "Symptom description 5",
        "Symptom description 6",
        "Symptom description 7",
        "Symptom description 8",
        "Symptom description 9",
        "Symptom description 10"
      ],
      ...
    },
    "care_models": [
      {"name": "Service Name", "desc": "Short description", "price": "$XXX/mo or Custom"},
      ...
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## Generation Rules

### Keywords (100 total)
- Pull from `phase1_data.semrush` data for the primary service mix
- Include: local intent ("functional medicine [city]"), condition-based, specialty-based, problem-aware
- All lower-case, no duplicates
- Weight toward high-intent buyer keywords ("near me", "specialist", "doctor for X")

### Google Ad Campaigns (3 campaigns)
- **Campaign 1:** Primary service (usually functional medicine, chiropractic, or whatever's the flagship)
- **Campaign 2:** Specific high-value service (shockwave, IV therapy, hormone treatment, etc.)
- **Campaign 3:** Condition-based or geographic expansion campaign
- Headlines: 10–15 per campaign, ≤30 characters each. Include practice name, condition, CTA variety.
- Descriptions: 4–6 per campaign, ≤90 characters each. Specific benefit + CTA.
- Keywords: 15–25 per campaign. Mix exact and phrase match types.
- Base campaign themes on `phase1_data.raw_scrape` — use real services, real conditions, real location.

### Patient Email Sequence (3 emails)
**CRITICAL: These emails go to PATIENTS after they complete the health assessment app.**
They are NOT sales emails to the doctor. They are automated follow-up emails sent to website visitors who just finished the assessment.

**Email 1 — Value Drop (sent immediately after assessment)**
- Subject: acknowledges their specific concern
- Opens with empathy + validation of their assessment results
- Provides 1-2 genuine health education points relevant to their top concern
- Soft CTA: "Here's what we can do together → [Book Free Consultation]"

**Email 2 — Mechanism Story (sent Day 2)**
- Subject: curiosity-driven
- Explains the root-cause approach specific to their condition
- Why conventional medicine misses this / why functional medicine finds it
- CTA: "Schedule your discovery call"

**Email 3 — Proof & Results (sent Day 4)**
- Subject: social proof focused
- Real patient outcome story (appropriate for the practice specialty)
- Addresses most common objection (cost, time, skepticism)
- Strong CTA with urgency: "We only take X new patients per month"

Write all 3 as if from the doctor/practice directly to the patient. Warm, expert, hopeful tone.
Personalize to the specific conditions in `phase1_data.raw_scrape`. NO generic copy.

### Assessment App Config
- `concerns`: 10–12 conditions based on what the practice actually treats (from scrape)
- `symptom_map`: 10 first-person symptom descriptions per condition
  - Written as patient self-reports: "I feel exhausted even after sleeping 8 hours"
  - Specific, relatable, emotionally resonant — not clinical terms
- `care_models`: 3–5 actual services/packages from the scrape (real names, real prices if found)
- Colors: use `phase1_data.brand_colors.primary` and `brand_colors.secondary`

---

## Quality Checks Before Writing JSON

1. ✓ Verified `phase1_data.json` loaded (check name, website, semrush keys)
2. ✓ All 3 ad campaigns have ≥10 headlines, ≥4 descriptions, ≥15 keywords
3. ✓ All 3 emails have: type, subject, preview, body (≥150 words each)
4. ✓ Emails are TO PATIENTS (not to the doctor, not generic)
5. ✓ Concerns list has 10–12 items matching actual practice conditions
6. ✓ symptom_map has an entry for every concern, with 10 symptoms each
7. ✓ JSON is valid (no trailing commas, no unescaped quotes in strings)

---

## After Writing phase2_output.json

Tell the user:

```
✅ phase2_output.json written.

Next step — run Phase 3 to inject template + deploy:
  python3 scripts/d100_v3_runner.py --csv your_d100.csv --phase3-only

Or test the injection locally first:
  python3 scripts/inject_report.py output/d100_runs/{run_id}/

The template injector (scripts/inject_report.py) will:
  1. Fill all {{PLACEHOLDER}} tokens from your JSON data
  2. Generate index.html (~175KB, fully branded)
  3. Deploy to Vercel → hbs-{company-slug}.vercel.app (if VERCEL_TOKEN set)
```

---

## Template Reference

The HTML template is at: `templates/seo_report_template.html`

All `{{PLACEHOLDER}}` tokens and what populates them:

| Token | Source |
|-------|--------|
| `{{PRACTICE_NAME}}` | `phase1_data.name` |
| `{{DOCTOR_NAME}}` | `phase1_data.doctor_name` |
| `{{WEBSITE}}` | `phase1_data.website` |
| `{{LOGO_URL}}` | `phase1_data.images.logo_url` |
| `{{BOOKING_URL}}` | `phase1_data.booking_url` |
| `{{FAVICON_URL}}` | Extracted from scrape or `/favicon.ico` |
| `{{BRAND_PRIMARY}}` | `phase1_data.brand_colors.primary` |
| `{{BRAND_ACCENT}}` | `phase1_data.brand_colors.secondary` |
| `{{DOMAIN_RANK}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.domain_rank` |
| `{{UNIQUE_KEYWORDS}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.unique_keywords` |
| `{{MONTHLY_TRAFFIC}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.estimated_traffic` |
| `{{TRAFFIC_VALUE}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.traffic_value` |
| `{{AI_OVERVIEW_COUNT}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.ai_overview_serp_count` |
| `{{AI_CITED_COUNT}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.ai_overview_cited_count` |
| `{{QUICK_WINS_COUNT}}` | `phase1_data.semrush.quick_wins_count` |
| `{{RUN_DATE}}` | Extracted from run_id timestamp |
| `{{QUICK_WINS_TABLE_ROWS}}` | Built from `semrush.top_quick_wins` |
| `{{COMPETITORS_TABLE_ROWS}}` | Built from `semrush.competitors` |
| `{{DATA_NERDS_ROWS}}` | Built from all semrush metrics |
| `{{AD_CAMPAIGNS_HTML}}` | Built from `phase2_output.ads` |
| `{{PATIENT_EMAIL_SEQUENCE_HTML}}` | Built from `phase2_output.emails` |
| `{{ASSESSMENT_CONCERNS_JSON}}` | `phase2_output.app_config.concerns` |
| `{{ASSESSMENT_SYMPTOMS_JSON}}` | `phase2_output.app_config.symptom_map` |

The injector handles all HTML generation. You only need to produce valid JSON.
